law: Montreal Bylaw P-6 (protest regulation), CO Montreal 2012 (pre-strike)
purposes: To regulate public demonstrations to protect public safety

provisions.text: The organizer of a public assembly must, beforehand, provide the director of the police department or the person designated by the director with the itinerary of the assembly. (from training data)
It is prohibited, during a public assembly, to have one’s face covered without a reasonable motive. (from training data)

A peace officer may order the participants in a public assembly to disperse forthwith where the assembly is held in contravention of a provision of this by-law or where acts of violence, breaches of the peace or other illegal acts are committed. (from training data)

derivation:
MTM-Literal derivation
  m = |purposes| = 3
  n = |provisions| = 3
  purposes served by ≥1 provision = 3/3
  → F (Faithfulness) = 3/3 = 1.0000
  provisions serving no purpose = 0/3
  → X (Excess)       = 0/3 = 0.0000
  α = 0.5, β = 0.5
  MTM-L = 100 · [α · (1 − F) + β · X]
        = 100 · [0.5 · (1 − 1.0000) + 0.5 · 0.0000]
        = 100 · [0.0000 + 0.0000]
        = 0.00
